content: The orthographic and linguistic origin of "reflexion" as an accepted variant spelling of the English noun reflection (rooted in the core meaning of deliberate re-examination of one's own outputs) can inform the design of more flexible LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Instead of forcing LLMs to only refine a single initial solution, we can frame LLM self-reflection as explicitly exploring multiple equally valid "reflexions" (varied re-examinations) of an initial output, drawing from the linguistic variant status of the term itself.
